hardcoded_secret.description: Hardcoded secret or credential detected in source code
hardcoded_secret.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables or use a dedicated secrets manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)

sql_injection.description: Possible SQL injection via string concatenation or formatting
sql_injection.remediation: Use parameterized queries or ORM methods instead of string concatenation

xss_pattern.remediation: Sanitize user input and use textContent instead of innerHTML, or use framework-provided escaping

command_injection.remediation: Use subprocess with list arguments, avoid shell=True, and validate all user inputs

path_traversal.remediation: Validate and sanitize file paths, use allowlists, and avoid using user input directly in file operations

insecure_random.description: Use of non-cryptographic random number generator in potentially security-sensitive context
insecure_random.remediation: Use secrets module (Python) or crypto.getRandomValues (JS) for security-sensitive randomness

debug_enabled.description: Debug mode appears to be enabled
debug_enabled.remediation: Disable debug mode in production deployments

weak_crypto.description: Use of weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithm
weak_crypto.remediation: Use SHA-256 or stronger hash functions, and AES-256 for encryption

insecure_deserialization.remediation: Avoid deserializing untrusted data. Use yaml.safe_load() instead of yaml.load(), avoid pickle for untrusted input

ssrf_pattern.description: Possible Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via user-controlled URL
ssrf_pattern.remediation: Validate and sanitize URLs, use allowlists for permitted domains, and avoid passing user input directly to HTTP request functions
